fields.description:
# Derive and enforce plan-material-pin health

## Goal
Use signed pins as the single related-material validity check across every attestation lifecycle consumer, while preserving safe rollout for existing projects.

## Implementation
- Add verify.enforce_plan_material_pins with default false and strict enabled-mode validation.
- Own the internal DerivedPlanReviewHealth contract with status exactly current, drifted, missing-target, malformed, legacy-unpinned, or enforcement-disabled plus canonical target details and enforced boolean. Compare signed pins against current narrow material to populate it; all renderers consume this contract rather than duplicating strings.
- When enabled, drift, malformed pins, or a deleted/missing pinned target invalidate the attestation. Archived targets remain readable. When disabled, report the same observations as advisory while valid remains true.
- Treat a wholly unpinned legacy attestation as valid in either mode. Do not rewrite pins when configuration changes.
- Own one compute_plan_review_validity profile boundary used by claim, reuse, drift refresh, re-sign, audit/health, and close. The claim profile checks code freshness. The close profile sets check_code_freshness=false while retaining signature, registry, phase, own-material, and configured pin checks; the close story must call this profile rather than reimplement validity.
- Keep optional unreadable configuration fail-open with a warning, matching existing gate posture; once enforcement is successfully enabled, validation fails closed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Enabled pin drift invalidates the claim consumer.
- [ ] Enabled pin drift invalidates the close-profile consumer when that gate is active.
- [ ] Enabled pin drift prevents idempotent attestation reuse.
- [ ] Enabled pin drift prevents progressive drift refresh.
- [ ] Enabled pin drift prevents re-sign.
- [ ] Audit reports enabled pin drift as invalid.
- [ ] Derived health reports enabled pin drift as invalid.
- [ ] Disabled enforcement reports the same drift as advisory without changing valid.
- [ ] Legacy unpinned attestations remain valid across configuration toggles.
- [ ] Missing/deleted, archived, malformed, duplicate, and re-enable scenarios are deterministic.
- [ ] No warning event is persisted for derived health.

## Verification
- Run focused attestation invalidation, reuse, drift-refresh, re-sign, and config tests.
- Run make check and the non-integration test suite.
